author: @peterdingle6959
text:
Hi Robert,
What does the microwave do in the reaction? You said leaving it in the microwave longer effects the purity and size of the magnetite, so the longer the bigger the particles?
Thanks, enjoy your videos!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: gives it energy - but it is directed specifically at the reaction site

replies.author: @peterdingle6959
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith  Ok, so a longer time in the microwave does what to the size and purity?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: this is beginning to sound like an experiment set for you to do lol - you know I encourage people to find their own answers mate - especially when i have no idea lol - so do it and let me know - i'd appreciate it and I am sure lots of others would

author: @hankywooky4222
text:
Dear !!! <3
Can you list me an ingredients of it and an amount of the material ?
Cuz I can't listen clearly from the video T.T

author: @serotonin67
text: Good until 1:43.   What is this washing process about?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: well, you have a load of junk in there - sodium hydroxide, iron sulphate and whatever impurities there were from the original material - after all it was from a garden center - and you have to get them out. Luckily magnetite is magnetic - so washing and magnetically separating the particles purifies them

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Good idea it will help control particle size. Basically a polyol synthesis.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: in order to create nanoparticles you would need to excercise a little more control over the reaction conditions. Specifically add 10% DMF to the solution or do it in a polyol like diethylene glycol.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Yes but I washed it and evaporated it first

author: @rx327prime
text:

Ask for someone to give you a compact camera, it will be in any way better than that, I'm sure someone will send you one.

author: @MongrelShark
text:

All I need is the iron sulfate!
